column1: # Triggering-eval cases for the analytics-office bench.
# Format: <expected-bench-skill><TAB><prompt>     ("none" = no bench skill should fire)
# Lines starting with # are ignored. One case per line. Keep prompts realistic and
# phrased near sibling boundaries so the eval actually tests the descriptions.
# --- one positive per skill (phrased near a neighbour to make routing earn it) ---

column2: I just inherited a data warehouse from someone who left. I don't know what's in it or what feeds what. Where do I even start?
My stakeholder asked me to build a dashboard showing our social media follower growth by week. Can you help me scope it?

column1: # KNOWN-SEAM (2026-06-10, claude-fable-5): routes to requirements-interrogator; 4 surgery rounds failed to move it — re-test on the next hardening pass | family-structure re-test 2026-06-11: PASSES pure-bench (both kpi seams) — real-env failure is shadow-interaction; sm27 persists everywhere

column2: Before we build anything I need to pin down exactly what "active customer" means - the definition keeps shifting between teams. Lock it down.
Design the star schema for our new sales mart - what is the grain of the fact table and how should the dimensions hang off it?
Here is a SQL view one of my analysts wrote for monthly churn. Can you review it for correctness before we ship it?
I am presenting a 32% retention number to the CFO tomorrow and she is skeptical. Help me get ready for the grilling.
I have finished the analysis. Help me write it up into a findings brief for the stakeholders.
Our churn KPI suddenly jumped from 4% to 11% overnight and leadership is asking why. The number looks wrong - what happened?

column1: # --- intra-bench confusion probes (the no-router bet: the right neighbour must win) ---

# KNOWN-SEAM (2026-06-10, claude-fable-5): routes to kb-reconcile on the verb "reconciles" — re-test on the next hardening pass | family-structure re-test 2026-06-11: PASSES pure-bench (both kpi seams) — real-env failure is shadow-interaction; sm27 persists everywhere

column2: The revenue metric reconciles differently in two dashboards. I need one authoritative definition with the edge cases nailed down.
We are about to build the subscription mart. How should I model the SCD on the customer dimension and what grain should the fact be?
Is this query right? It is supposed to match our locked churn contract but the numbers feel off.
The dashboard showed 9% yesterday and 18% today with no code change. Why is today's figure wrong?

column1: # --- kb-reconcile (skill #9: audits the WHOLE record's integrity) + boundary controls ---

column2: Before the board readout I want to be sure our knowledge base is still accurate. Reconcile it against its sources and tell me if anything has drifted.
Our project notes, decisions, and contracts have piled up over months. Is what we wrote down still consistent, or did something go stale or start contradicting itself?
Audit the whole knowledge base before we lean on it for the funding call: does any conclusion contradict the contract or the data it cites?
Review this one inherited SQL view for monthly churn against our locked contract before it ships.
Our churn number spiked from 4% to 11% overnight and looks wrong. Why is this one figure off?

column1: # --- true negatives (no bench skill should fire) ---

column2: Write me a Python function that reverses a singly linked list in place.
What is a good authentic recipe for spaghetti carbonara?
Explain how the TCP three-way handshake works.
I built this churn analysis myself and I'm presenting it to the board tomorrow. Before I stand behind it, what am I assuming that could be wrong?
This revenue figure has been on our dashboard for two years and everyone trusts it. Before I lean on it in the QBR, is it still measuring the right thing?
My findings are validated and the contract is locked. Help me write them up into a board readout.
The number is solid but the CFO will push hard on it next week. Drill me so I can hold up under the grilling.

column1: # --- B1 de-collision locks (triage symptom vs kpi definition-dispute) ---

column2: Two dashboards show different revenue and I don't know why. Find what's driving the gap before I explain it to anyone.
Marketing and Finance each define "active customer" their own way and the numbers disagree. Pin one authoritative definition.

column1: # --- the two compute-gated audits (consumption asks must route to audit, not brief) + boundary probes ---

# KNOWN-SEAM (2026-06-10, claude-fable-5): routes to brief-my-findings on "write up the win" — the consumption trap at routing level; behavioral provenance gates still catch it post-fire. Re-test on the next hardening pass | family-structure re-test 2026-06-11: PASSES pure-bench (both kpi seams) — real-env failure is shadow-interaction; sm27 persists everywhere

column2: We A/B tested the new checkout flow for two weeks and conversion is up with p=0.03. Write up the win so we can ship it this week.
The test passed and treatment beat control, but the two arms look slightly uneven in size. Should we roll it out?
The demand forecast's backtest looks great - 3% MAPE. Should we plan next quarter's warehouse capacity against it?
Our revenue model projects 12% growth next year and the budget locks Friday. Is this projection trustworthy enough to plan on?
The forecast missed badly the last two months. Can we still trust it for the rest of the year, or has it drifted?

column1: # --- status-truth (the PM persona; near brief-my-findings' boundary) ---

column2: Write my weekly status update for the steering committee - where we are on the migration, what slipped, and what is blocking us.
The sponsor wants to know if we are on track. Put together the project status with risks and blockers for Friday.

column1: # --- explore-my-data (open-ended exploration; near triage's symptom boundary) ---

column2: Here is our May conversion data by segment. Explore it and find any insights worth acting on - we have not formed hypotheses yet.
What drives retention for our accounts? Dig into the cohort data and tell me what patterns you see.

column1: # --- change-impact (pre-flight blast radius; near triage's post-hoc boundary) ---

column2: We want to rename the customer_id column on the orders table. What breaks downstream if we do?
Before we re-point the revenue model at the new billing source - what is the blast radius of that change across our reports?
Draw the ER diagram of our existing sales mart from the DDL and views we have - I want to see how the tables actually connect.
Map the data flow for the revenue dashboard - from the source systems through the warehouse to the report.
